question: [File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user perform a DNS poisoning or BGP hijacking attack against the unencrypted HTTP endpoint resolved from cfg.mirrorRest to return a poisoned nodesEnvelope, causing hiero.ClientForNetwork(netmap) to build a client pointing entirely to attacker-controlled gRPC endpoints, so that all subsequent CryptoTransfer transactions are submitted to nodes that accept but never forward to consensus, permanently freezing operator funds in limbo?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user perform a network-level MITM attack on the plaintext HTTP connection to cfg.mirrorRest (http.Client has no TLS enforcement) and inject a nodesEnvelope response containing only attacker-controlled service_endpoints, so that hiero.ClientForNetwork(netmap) routes every AddHbarTransfer transaction to a black-hole node, causing permanent fund freeze without any on-chain error?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls a rogue mirror node REST server (reachable via cfg.mirrorRest=other) return a nodesEnvelope where every nodeEntry has a valid node_account_id but service_endpoints pointing to a TCP server that accepts gRPC connections but never responds, causing cryptoTransfer.Execute(client) to block indefinitely and freeze all in-flight HBAR?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of pagination following in buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes (Links.Next is parsed but never fetched) by controlling a mirror node that returns an empty first page of nodes, causing fetchMirrorNodeNetwork to return 'no usable service_endpoints found', preventing newClient from initializing and permanently halting all fund transfers?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user who can influence the mirror node REST response return a nodesEnvelope where all nodeEntry.ServiceEndpoints have Port == 0, causing every endpoint to be skipped by the 'ep.Port == 0' guard in fetchMirrorNodeNetwork, resulting in an empty network map, a failed newClient call, and permanent cessation of fund transfers?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user who can influence the mirror node REST response return a nodesEnvelope where all nodeEntry.ServiceEndpoints have both DomainName and IPAddressV4 as empty strings, causing every endpoint to be skipped by the host-empty guard in fetchMirrorNodeNetwork, producing an empty netmap, and causing hiero.ClientForNetwork(netmap) to build a client with no reachable nodes, permanently freezing all fund movement?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that fetchMirrorNodeNetwork only reads the first page of mirror node results (no pagination loop over Links.Next) by controlling a mirror node that returns a first page with only malformed node_account_id strings (causing hiero.AccountIDFromString to fail and skip all entries), resulting in an empty network map and a permanently non-functional client?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user who can perform DNS rebinding against the cfg.mirrorRest hostname cause fetchMirrorNodeNetwork to initially resolve to a legitimate mirror node (passing any network-level checks) and then rebind to an attacker-controlled server that returns a poisoned nodesEnvelope, so that hiero.ClientForNetwork(netmap) is built with attacker-controlled gRPC endpoints and all HBAR transfers are permanently misdirected?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of response body size limit in json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&payload) within fetchMirrorNodeNetwork by serving an extremely large JSON payload from a controlled mirror node, causing OOM or extreme latency that prevents newClient from completing, thereby freezing all fund transfers indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror node REST response inject a nodeEntry with a node_account_id that parses successfully via hiero.AccountIDFromString but refers to a non-existent or frozen consensus node account, causing hiero.ClientForNetwork(netmap) to build a client that routes all CryptoTransfer transactions to an invalid node, resulting in permanent transaction rejection and fund freeze?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that cfg.operatorKey defaults to a publicly known hardcoded value ('302e020100300506032b65700422042091132178e72057a1d7528025956fe39b0b847f200ab59b2fdd367017f3087137') to submit competing transactions from the same operator account ID, causing DUPLICATE_TRANSACTION or INVALID_ACCOUNT_ID errors that permanently block the pinger's fund transfers?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user who can influence the mirror node REST response return a nodesEnvelope with a single nodeEntry whose service_endpoints point to a gRPC server that returns INVALID_NODE_ACCOUNT for every transaction, causing submitWithRetry to exhaust all maxRetries attempts on every tick and permanently fail to transfer funds?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of TLS certificate pinning or CA validation in the http.Client used by buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes to present a fraudulent TLS certificate (via a compromised CA or self-signed cert accepted by the system trust store) and serve a poisoned nodesEnvelope, redirecting all HBAR transfers to attacker-controlled nodes and permanently freezing funds?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror node REST response return a nodesEnvelope where all service_endpoints use IPv6 addresses in the IPAddressV4 field, causing net.JoinHostPort to produce malformed addresses that the hiero SDK cannot connect to, resulting in an effectively empty or non-functional network map and permanent fund freeze?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes constructs the URL by string concatenation (base + '/api/v1/network/nodes') without validating cfg.mirrorRest for path traversal or URL injection, to redirect the HTTP request to an internal service that returns a crafted nodesEnvelope, poisoning the network map used by hiero.ClientForNetwork(netmap)?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user who can influence the mirror node REST response return a nodesEnvelope with duplicate entries for the same gRPC address mapped to different node_account_id values, causing hiero.ClientForNetwork(netmap) to overwrite earlier entries and build a client that routes all transactions to a single attacker-controlled node, permanently freezing funds?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the retry logic in buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes (which retries on HTTP 500/503 but not on malformed JSON) by serving a mirror node that returns valid HTTP 200 with a JSON payload containing a nodesEnvelope with all nodes having invalid node_account_id strings, causing hiero.AccountIDFromString to fail for every entry, producing an empty network map with no retry, and permanently preventing newClient from succeeding?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user who can perform ARP spoofing on the local network segment where the pinger runs intercept the HTTP request to cfg.mirrorRest and return a nodesEnvelope with attacker-controlled gRPC endpoints, causing hiero.ClientForNetwork(netmap) to route all CryptoTransfer transactions to a node that silently drops them, permanently freezing operator HBAR?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that cfg.network='other' causes newClient to call buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes which has no authentication on the mirror node REST endpoint, allowing any unauthenticated server at cfg.mirrorRest to supply a nodesEnvelope that maps all transactions to a single attacker-controlled gRPC endpoint, causing permanent fund freeze?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror node REST response return a nodesEnvelope where grpc_proxy_endpoint is populated but service_endpoints is empty for every node, exploiting the fact that the code only iterates over ServiceEndpoints (not GrpcProxyEndpoint), resulting in an empty network map and a permanently non-functional hiero.ClientForNetwork(netmap)?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that hiero.PrivateKeyFromString(cfg.operatorKey) accepts multiple key formats (ED25519, ECDSA, DER-encoded) without format validation in newClient, and by influencing cfg.operatorKey to a syntactically valid but semantically wrong key type, cause client.SetOperator to configure an operator whose signatures are always rejected by consensus nodes, permanently freezing all fund transfers?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user who can influence the mirror node REST response return a nodesEnvelope with service_endpoints containing hostnames that resolve to 127.0.0.1 or internal RFC-1918 addresses, causing hiero.ClientForNetwork(netmap) to build a client that routes all CryptoTransfer transactions to loopback/internal addresses that reject gRPC connections, permanently freezing funds?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a maximum timeout on the overall newClient initialization (buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes uses cfg.mirrorNodeClientTimeout per request but not for the total initialization) by controlling a mirror node that responds just within the per-request timeout but serves an infinite stream of paginated results, causing newClient to hang indefinitely and never initialize, permanently halting fund transfers?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user who can influence the mirror node REST response return a nodesEnvelope with a node_account_id of '0.0.0' (the zero account), which parses successfully via hiero.AccountIDFromString but is not a valid consensus node, causing hiero.ClientForNetwork(netmap) to build a client that routes all transactions to an invalid node and permanently freezes funds?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the http.Client in buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes follows HTTP redirects by default, by controlling a mirror node that issues a 301/302 redirect to an attacker-controlled server serving a poisoned nodesEnvelope, causing hiero.ClientForNetwork(netmap) to be built with attacker-controlled gRPC endpoints and permanently freezing all HBAR transfers?
